Human.abilityText: None. Humans are wide spread across the galaxy, and each with their own strengths and weaknesses.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Humans gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

traits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
traits.description: Humans are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Human character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

Aqualish.abilityText: Aqualish receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both Wisdom and Charisma. Aqualish are hardy and fit, but are brash and brutish compared to others.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Rugged and resilient, Aqualish gain Toughness as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Aqualish can't drown in water.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: An Aqualish may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Aqualish may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

Bothan.abilityText: All Bothans receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Bothans are agile but not particularly resilient.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Bothans are natural spies and investigators. A Bothan with Gather Information as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Gather Information) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Bothans have above-average willpower and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

Cerean.abilityText: Cereans have a +2 bonus to both their Intelligence and their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Cereans are bright and intuitive but less coordinated than most other species.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Cerean with Initiative as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Initiative) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Intuitive Initiative
traits.description: Despite their general lack of coordination, Cereans' reaction speed is superior to that of most other Species. A Cerean may choose to reroll any Initiative check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

Duros.abilityText: Duros have a +2 bonus to both Dexterity and Intelligence, and suffer a -2 penalty to Constitution. Duros are agile in mind and body but they aren't physically hardy people.

traits.name: Expert Pilot
traits.description: As natural spacers, a Duros may choose to reroll any Pilot check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

Ewok.abilityText: All Ewoks receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Ewoks are very agile but somewhat weak.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Ewoks are skilled foragers. An Ewok with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Ewoks do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Ewoks have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Ewoks ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

traits.name: Sneaky
traits.description: An Ewok may choose to reroll any Stealth check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

Gamorrean.abilityText: Gamorreans have a +2 bonus to Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to both their Dexterity and Intelligence. Gamorreans possess greater physical power, but have limited agility and intelligence.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Gamorreans gain Improved Damage Threshold as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Great Fortitude
traits.description: Gamorreans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense, accounting for their great physical resistance.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Gamorreans do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity (Arg'garok)
traits.description: Gamorreans treat the Arg'garok as an Advanced Melee Weapon, instead of an Exotic Weapons (Melee).

Gungan.abilityText: Gungans have a natural +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer -2 penalties to their Intelligence and Charisma. Gungans are agile, but aren't particularly witty or astute.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Gungan may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse. In addition, a Gungan may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.description: Gungans are at home either in air or in water. A Gungan can hold his or her breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times their Constitution score before they need to make Endurance checks to hold their breath.

traits.name: Lightning Reflexes
traits.description: Gungans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense, accounting for their uncanny ability to avoid danger.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Gungans ignore Concealment (But not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Gungans treat the Atlatl, Cesta, and Electropole as Simple Weapons (Melee) instead of Exotic Weapons (Melee).

Hutt.abilityText: All Hutts receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, Constitution, and Intelligence; but suffer a -6 penalty to their Dexterity. Hutts are strong, hardy, and crafty; but are sluggish and uncoordinated.

traits.name: Force Resistance
traits.description: A Hutt's sharp mind allows them to resist Force abilities. Hutts receive a +5 bonus to their Will Defense against any use of the Use the Force skill.

traits.name: Supreme Stability
traits.description: A Hutt's sluglike body structure and lack of any kind of legs, allows a Hutt to become completely immune to being knocked Prone.

traits.name: Silver Tongued
traits.description: Hutts may be known to be heavy handed in their ventures, but they are equally capable in legal negotiations as they are underground business. A Hutt may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, keeping the better of the two results.

Ithorian.abilityText: All Ithorians receive a +2 bonus to both their Wisdom and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Ithorians are wise and deliberate thinkers, whose personable nature allows them to get along well with others, but they are not agile.

traits.name: Bellow
traits.description: As a Standard Action, an Ithorian can open all four of its throats and emit a terrible subsonic Bellow. The Ithorian makes a special attack roll (1d20 + its Character Level) and compares the result to the Fortitude Defense of all creatures and unattended objects in a 6-Square Cone. A successful hit deals 3d6 points of Sonic damage; if the attack misses, the target takes half damage instead. Each use of this ability moves the Ithorian -1 step along the Condition Track. An Ithorian can choose to add more dice (d6s) to the damage it deals with its Bellow, but each additional 1d6 of damage moves the Ithorian another -1 step along the Condition Track.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Ithorians like to maintain close ties with nature. An Ithorian with Knowledge (Life Sciences) as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Knowledge (Life Sciences)) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Ithorians have above-average willpower and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

traits.name: Survival Instinct
traits.description: An Ithorian may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Kel Dor.abilityText: All Kel Dor receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Kel Dor are quick and wise, but they suffer from weaker constitutions than others.

traits.name: Keen Force Sense
traits.description: Kel Dor may choose to reroll any Use the Force check made to Search Your Feelings or Sense Force, keeping the better of the two results.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Kel Dor ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Special Equipment
traits.description: Kel Dor suffer limitations outside their native atmosphere. Without protective goggles, a Kel Dor is considered Blind. Without an Antiox Breath Mask, a Kel Dor begins to Suffocate. A replacement mask costs 2,000 credits (500 on Dorin), and a year's supply of filters costs 200 credits (50 on Dorin). Kel Dor characters begin play with these items at no cost. An Antiox Breath Mask is poisonous to other Species.

Mon Calamari.abilityText: All Mon Calamari receive +2 bonuses to their Intelligence and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Mon Calamari are bright and prudent, but they have relatively frail physiques.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Mon Calamari can't drown in water.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Keenly perceptive, a Mon Calamari with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Mon Calamari may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Mon Calamari may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Mon Calamari ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Quarren.abilityText: Quarren receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but take -2 penalties to both Wisdom and Charisma. Quarren are extremely resilient and physically tough, though they tend toward deficiencies in wisdom and social graces.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Quarren can't drown in water.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Adept traders and negotiators, Quarren with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Quarren may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Quarren may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Quarren ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Rodian.abilityText: All Rodians receive a +2 bonus to Dexterity, but they suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Rodians are quick and well coordinated, but often act before thinking things through, and are often rude and try to bully others.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Rodians are taught how to track and survive in the wilderness of Rodia from a very young age. A Rodian with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: A Rodian may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Rodians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Sullustan.abilityText: Sullustans receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Sullustans are quick, agile, and good with ranged weapons, but they are not as hardy as other Species.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Sullustans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Expert Climber
traits.description: Sullustans are adept at climbing in their underground homes. A Sullustan may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: A Sullustan may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Trandoshan.abilityText: All Trandoshans receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Trandoshans are powerful but lack agility.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Rugged and resilient, Trandoshans receive the Toughness feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Trandoshans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Limb Regeneration
traits.description: A Trandoshan regrows a lost limb in 1d10 days. At the end of that time, all Persistent Conditions associated with the loss of the limb are removed.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Trandoshans have thick scales that provide a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

Twi'lek.abilityText: All Twi'leks receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Twi'leks make convincing diplomats, but are relatively weak-willed.

traits.name: Deceptive
traits.description: Naturally gifted at manipulation, a Twi'lek may choose to reroll any Deception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Great Fortitude
traits.description: Twi'leks gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense. Twi'leks have great health with a natural resistance to toxins and illnesses.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Twi'leks ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Wookiee.abilityText: All Wookiees receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, and +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity, Wisdom, and Charisma. Wookiees are powerful and hardy, but they aren't agile, tend to be impulsive, and have little patience for diplomacy.

traits.name: Expert Climber
traits.description: Wookiees are great climbers and may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Extraordinary Recuperation
traits.description: A Wookiee regains Hit Points at double the normal rate (see Natural Healing).

traits.name: Intimidating Presence
traits.description: Known to pull off the ears of Gundarks, Wookiees may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate others, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Rage
traits.description: Once per day, a Wookiee can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While Raging, the Wookiee temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Wookiee's Constitution modifier. At the end of its Rage, the Wookiee moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Wookiee takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Wookiee can't engage in any strenuous activity.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity (Bowcaster and Ryyk Blade)
traits.description: Wookiees treat the Bowcaster as a Rifle instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged). They additionally treat the Ryyk Blade as an Advanced Melee Weapon instead of an Exotic Weapon (Melee).

Zabrak.abilityText: Zabrak have no Ability Score adjustments.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: Having strong survival instincts and quick reactions, a Zabrak may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Superior Defenses
traits.description: Adapted to a very tough and trying environment, Zabrak gain a +1 Species bonus to all their Defenses.

Chistori.abilityText: All Chistori receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Chistori are strong, but their bodies are slow and sometimes ponderous.

traits.name: Cold-Blooded
traits.description: Chistori are very sensitive to cold climates. A Chistori takes a -5 penalty to their Fortitude Defense to resist Extreme Cold.

traits.name: Damage Reduction
traits.description: Chistori have thick scales that provide Damage Reduction 2.

traits.name: Rage
traits.description: Once per day, a Chistori can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While raging, the Chistori temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Chistori's Constitution modifier. At the end of it's rage, the Chistori moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Chistori takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Chistori can't engage in any strenuous activity.

Mantellian Savrip.abilities: +4 Str, +4 Con, -4 Int, -4 Wis, -4 Cha, -2 Dex
Mantellian Savrip.abilityText: All Mantellian Savrips receive a +4 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -4 penalties to their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma, as well as a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Mantellian Savrips are solid walls of muscle and power, but are unsophisticated and quick to anger.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Mantellian Savrips have a tough hide that protects them from harm. They gain a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Mantellian Savrip has Natural Weapons, in the form of tough claws. When a Mantellian Savrip makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Mantellian Savrips are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Poison
traits.description: If the Natural Weapons of a Mantellian Savrip can deal damage to a living target, the target is also Poisoned. If the Poison succeeds on an attack roll (1d20 + the Mantellian Savrip's Character Level) against the target's Fortitude Defense, the target moves -1 step along the Condition Track. A target moved to the end of the Condition Track by the Poison is Immobilized, but not Unconscious.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Mantellian Savrips do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Rage
traits.description: Once per day, a Mantellian Savrip can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While Raging, the Mantellian Savrip temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Mantellian Savrip's Constitution modifier. At the end of its Rage, the Mantellian Savrip moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Mantellian Savrip takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Mantellian Savrip can't engage in any strenuous activity.

Replica Droid.abilityText: All Replica Droids receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Replica Droids are built to be physically strong, but have to work to fit in seamlessly with organic beings. Additionally, like other Droids, Replica Droids have no Constitution score.

traits.name: Bonus Equipment
traits.description: Replica Droids can be constructed with two of the following Droid Systems installed: Internal Comlink, Darkvision, Diagnostics Package, Improved Sensor Package, Internal Storage (subject to size limitations), Translator Unit (DC 15).

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Seeking to perfectly imitate organic creatures, a Replica Droid that has Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Droid Traits
traits.description: Replica Droids operate as if they are 4th-Degree Droids, except that they ignore the Maintenance requirement. Replica Droids have all Droid Immunities and normal Droid Traits.

Utai.abilityText: All Utai receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Utai are sturdy and hard-working, but they do not make good leaders.

traits.name: Animal Handler
traits.description: The Utai are naturally gifted when it comes to dealing with Beasts and wild creatures. An Utai can choose to reroll any Ride check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. Additionally, an Utai can make Persuasion checks to change the Attitude of an undomesticated creature that has an Intelligence score of 2 or lower.

traits.name: Cooperative
traits.description: An Utai automatically succeeds when attempting to Aid Another on a Skill Check, as long as the target is adjacent to, and can hear and understand, the Utai.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Utai ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Arkanian.abilityText: All Arkanians receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Arkanians are well learned and value knowledge above all else, but their arrogant attitudes tends to grate on people nerves very quickly.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Arkanians ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Flash of Genius
traits.description: Arkanians have moments of brilliance. Once per encounter, an Arkanian can add a +5 circumstance bonus to to the result of one Knowledge, Mechanics, or Use Computer skill check.

traits.name: Intellectual Reputation
traits.description: Arkanian prominence in the galaxy makes many individuals more willing to go along with their ideas. An Arkanian can use their Intelligence modifier in place of their Charisma modifier on Persuasion checks.

Arkanian Offshoot.abilityText: All Arkanian Offshoots either have a +2 bonus to their Dexterity or Strength depending on their genetic engineering, but all suffer from a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Arkanian Offshoots have been genetically modified to excel at physical task, but that genetic tampering was weakened their physique, making them frail and sickly.

abilityChoice.description: All Arkanian Offshoots either have a +2 bonus to their Dexterity or Strength depending on their genetic engineering, but all suffer from a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Arkanian Offshoots have been genetically modified to excel at physical task, but that genetic tampering was weakened their physique, making them frail and sickly.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Arkanian Offshoots are typically engineered for specific industrial tasks. They gain the Skill Focus Feat for one of the following Skills in which they are Trained: Endurance, Mechanics, Pilot, Survival, or Treat Injury.

traits.name: Determination
traits.description: Arkanian Offshoots are particularly determined and driven. Once per encounter, an Arkanian Offshoot can reroll one failed Skill Check with a Trained Skill. This reroll can be declared after the success or failure of the roll is known. The Arkanian Offshoot must take the result of the reroll, even if it is worse.

Cathar.abilityText: All Cathar receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Cathar have fast reflexes and are light on their feet, but often focus less on academics.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skills
traits.description: A Cathar's inherent instincts make them naturally agile and silent, even without specialized training. A Cathar treats the Climb and Stealth skills as Class Skills, regardless of Heroic Class.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Cathar has Natural Weapons, in the form of razor-sharp claws. When a Cathar makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Cathar are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Reactive Claw
traits.description: Once per encounter when a Cathar damages an opponent with a melee attack, the Cathar can make a single melee attack with its Natural Weapons against that target as a Swift Action. The Cathar must have one hand free at the time to make this attack (and thus cannot be wielding a two-handed weapon).

Draethos.abilityText: All Draethos receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Draethos have a hardy and durable structure, but due to their foreign and alien nature have trouble integrating socially.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Draethos gain Weapon Proficiency (Advanced Melee Weapons) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Draethos ignore Concealment from darkness (but not Total Concealment).

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: The Daethos's tough skin provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense.

traits.name: Natural Telepath
traits.description: A Draethos gains a +5 Species bonus using the Telepathy aspect of the Use the Force skill and can make Untrained Use the Force checks to use this application even without the Force Sensitivity feat.

Feeorin.abilityText: All Feeorin receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity, Wisdom, and Charisma. Feeorin are big and brawny, but tend to be blunt and short-tempered.

traits.name: Brutal
traits.description: Feeorin are vicious combatants who do not hesitate to strike vulnerable areas or fight dirty. Once per encounter, after making a successful melee attack, a Feeorin may treat its opponent's Damage Threshold as though it were 5 points lower when determining the result of the attack.

traits.name: Great Fortitude
traits.description: Feeorin gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense, accounting for their great physical condition.

traits.name: Inborn Resilience
traits.description: Feeorin are highly resilient to physical attack. A Feeorin can catch a Second Wind even when unconscious (no Action required, but the Second Wind can only be taken when the Feeorin would normally get a turn). Catching a Second Wind while unconscious also move the Feeorin +3 steps on the Condition Track.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Feeorin ignore Concealment from darkness (but not Total Concealment).

traits.name: Stronger by Age
traits.description: At Middle Age, a Feeorin adds a +1 Species bonus to their Strength and Constitution scores. At Old, this bonus increases to +2. Feeorin do not increase their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma; nor decrease their Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution as they Age.

Khil.abilityText: Khil receive +2 bonuses to their Intelligence and Charisma, but take a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Khil are charming and well-learned, but their endurance leaves much to be desired.

traits.name: Cooperative Spirit
traits.description: Khil work with others easily. They can Aid Another on Skill Checks made by adjacent allies as a Swift Action, and others can likewise aid Khil on Skill Checks as Swift Actions when adjacent to the Khil. This ability only applies to Climb, Endurance, Gather Information, Jump, Knowledge, Mechanics, Pilot, Ride, Survival, Swim, Treat Injury, or Use Computer skill checks.

traits.name: Dependable Worker
traits.description: Khil reliably achieve good results. Once per encounter, a Khil can treat one Skill Check with certain Skills (Climb, Endurance, Gather Information, Jump, Knowledge, Mechanics, Pilot, Ride, Survival, Swim, Treat Injury, or Use Computer) as though he or she had Taken 10, even after the roll has been made (but before the result of the check is known).

traits.name: Self-Sustaining
traits.description: Khil are able to absorb nutrition through the tentacles on their faces, called hullepi, drawing nourishment from almost any environment. Khil do not suffer the effect of Starvation, except in sterile environments.

Kissai.abilityText: All Kissai receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Kissai are charming and pleasant, but their devotion to The Sith blinds them to the suffering that follows.

traits.name: Inspired
traits.description: Kissai make the best use of any inspiration or information provided to them. Whenever a Kissai receives a morale or insight bonus from any source, the bonus increases by +1.

traits.name: Warrior's Awareness
traits.description: Kissai grow up in a violent and warlike society, so they constantly watch for threats. Once per encounter as a Reaction, a Kissai can gain the benefit of the Uncanny Dodge I and Uncanny Dodge II Talents until the beginning of the Kissai's next turn.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Kissai treat the Massassi Lanvarok as a Simple Weapon (Ranged) instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

Massassi.abilityText: All Massassi receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. Massassi are brawny and powerful, but lack the patience and mental strength of their Kissai cousins.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Massassi are skilled trackers. A Massassi with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Duty Bound
traits.description: Once per encounter when a Massassi receives a morale bonus of any kind from any source, the Massassi gains Bonus Hit Points equal to 4 x the Massassi's Character Level until the end of the encounter.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Massassi do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Warrior's Awareness
traits.description: Massassi grow up in a violent and warlike society, so they constantly watch for threats. Once per encounter as a Reaction, a Massassi can gain the benefit of the Uncanny Dodge I and Uncanny Dodge II Talents until the beginning of the Massassi's next turn.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Massassi treat the Massassi Lanvarok as a Simple Weapon (Ranged) instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

Miraluka.abilityText: All Miraluka receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Miraluka have keen minds, but their reliance on The Force to perceive their surroundings prevents them from reacting as quickly as most.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Miraluka who has Use the Force as a Trained Skill gains Force Training as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Force Sight
traits.description: Although blind, Miraluka can "see" by using The Force. They act normally, without taking any penalties for blindness when in the presence of living creatures, or life of any kind. This trait enables them to make Perception checks, and read and write as a sighted person. They can ignore the effect of darkness as if they had Darkvision. Miraluka cannot, however, detect creatures that are "invisible" to The Force such as the Yuuzhan Vong and the Ysalamiri. These creatures have Total Concealment from Miraluka, as do creatures in areas the dampen or suppress The Force. Additionally, Miraluka ignore all penalties to Perception checks from Cover and Concealment (but not Total Cover or Total Concealment). Miraluka do not increase the DC of Use the Force checks made to Sense Surroundings against targets with Total Cover.

Rakata.abilityText: All Rakata receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Rakata are crafty and intelligent, but their pride can blind them to their mistakes.

traits.name: Ancient Knowledge
traits.description: Rakata retain many of the qualities that led their ancestors to create great technological works in the distant past. Rakata reduce the penalty for not being proficient in a weapon from -5 to -2, and reduce the Armor Check Penalty for Armor they are not proficient in by one step (-5 for Heavy Armor, -2 for Medium Armor, and no penalty for Light Armor). All other penalties for not being proficient still apply. Additionally, once per encounter the Rakata can use a Trained-only application of the Mechanics, Pilot, Treat Injury, or Use Computer skills, even if Untrained in that skill.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Rakata with Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons) or proficient with any Exotic Weapon (Melee) gain the Weapon Focus feat (for Simple Weapons or a chosen Exotic Weapon (Melee)) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Force Blind
traits.description: For reasons unknown, the Rakata have lost their connection to The Force. Rakata cannot take the Force Sensitivity feat. They can still increase their Dark Side Score and are still affected by Force Powers, Force Talents, and the Use the Force skill as normal.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Rakata do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Rage
traits.description: Once per day, a Rakata can fly into a Rage as a Swift Action. While raging, the Rakata temporarily gains a +2 Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls, but cannot use Skills that require patience and concentration, such as Mechanics, Stealth, or Use the Force. A fit of Rage lasts for a number of rounds equal to 5 + the Rakata's Constitution modifier. At the end of its rage, the Rakata moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this Persistent Condition persist until the Rakata takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the Rakata can't engage in any strenuous activity.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Rakata do not treat Rakatan Weapons as Exotic Weapons, instead treating them as a base item (for instance, a Rakatan Blaster Rifle would still be treated as a Rifle).

Selkath.abilityText: All Selkath receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma. Most Selkath tend to be skilled in talking their way out of an argument, without taking a side.

traits.name: Able Healers
traits.description: Whenever a Selkath restores Hit Points to another character by any means, or Aids Another who is doing so, the target regains an additional 5 Hit Points. Additionally, once per encounter as a Swift Action, the Selkath can grant an adjacent ally Bonus Hit Points equal to 10 + the Selkath's Character Level, which last until the end of the encounter.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Selkath can't drown in water.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Selkath may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Selkath may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

Snivvian.abilityText: All Snivvians receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom. Snivvians tend to be thoughtful and observant of their surroundings.

traits.name: Cold Resistance
traits.description: The Snivvians' thick skin provides protection from extreme cold. Snivvians gain a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense to resist Extreme Cold.

traits.name: Observant
traits.description: Snivvians are highly aware of their surroundings, giving them an advantage when dealing with others. Once per encounter, a Snivvian can use their Perception skill modifier in place of their Deception or Persuasion skill modifier.

Aleena.abilityText: All Aleena receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Aleena are extremely quick, but they have difficulty relating to other Species.

traits.name: Energy Surge
traits.description: Once per encounter, an Aleena can gain a temporary boost to quickness and reflexes. When this ability is activated as a Swift Action, an Aleena gains a +2 bonus on Dexterity-based Skill Checks, and increases its base speed to 8 squares. The energy lasts for a number of rounds equal to the Aleena's Constitution modifier (minimum 1 round). When the energy is expended, the Aleena moves -1 Persistent step along the Condition Track. The penalties imposed by this condition persist until the Aleena takes at least 10 minutes to recuperate, during which time the creature can't engage in any strenuous activity.

traits.name: Heightened Agility
traits.description: Aleena are quite adept at avoiding danger in combat when they can see it coming. An Aleena can choose to reroll any Acrobatics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Caamasi.abilityText: All Caamasi receive a +4 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Strength and Constitution. Caamasi are renowned for their insight, not their physical strength.

traits.name: Memory Sharing
traits.description: Caamasi can create strong, lasting memories and share them with others of their Species through an inborn telepathy. Sharing a memory begins with taking a Full-Round Action to initiate the link between two adjacent Caamasi; transferring a memory requires as much time as the events being recalled. The recipient of a memory must be willing to receive it. Experiencing Memniis not like receiving a photographic memory of those events, but rather a complete, immersive experience. Any character who receives a Memnis from a Caamasi always gains favorable circumstances on Knowledge checks related to that memory. Force-using characters can also receive Memnii from a Caamasi with a successful DC 15 Use the Force check, made as a Reaction.

traits.name: Pacifism
traits.description: As a Standard Action, a Caamasi can make a Persuasion check against the Will Defense of a single target within line of sight. If the check is successful, the target moves -1 step along the Condition Track if it makes any attacks before the start of the Caamasi's next turn. The target must be able to hear and understand the Caamasi for this ability to have any effect, and any single target can be affected only once at any given time (meaning that multiple Caamasi may not use this ability on the same target at the same time). This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

traits.name: Silver Tongued
traits.description: Caamasi are skilled negotiators, and patient consulars. A Caamasi can choose to reroll any Persuasion check, using the better result.

Felucian.abilityText: Felucians receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Felucians must be tough to compete with the dangerous plants and animals of their homeworld, but they live in a relatively primitive state.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Felucians receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Felucians can't drown in water.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Felucians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Natural Camouflage
traits.description: A Felucian can choose to reroll any Stealth check to Sneak, keeping the better of the two results.

traits.name: Force Blast
traits.description: Once per encounter a Felucian can make an Untrained Use the Force check to activate the Force Blast Force Power. A Felucian who takes the Force Training feat automatically adds a single use of the Force Blast Force Power to his or her Force Power Suite (for free) instead of this ability.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Felucians do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Felucians treat the Felucian Skullblade as a Simple Weapon (Melee) instead of an Exotic Weapon (Melee).

Gran.abilityText: All Gran receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Gran are highly social creatures, but tend to value artistic value higher than scientific achievement.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Gran with the Point-Blank Shot feat gains the Precise Shot feat as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Gran ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Innate Accuracy
traits.description: Once per encounter, a Gran can Aim by taking a single Swift Action instead of two Swift Actions.

traits.name: Target Awareness
traits.description: A Gran takes no distance penalties on Perception checks made to notice targets for the first 50 squares between the Gran and its targets.

Noghri.abilityText: All Noghri receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Wisdom, but suffer a -4 penalty to their Charisma. Noghri are quick and wise, but serious and not very personable.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Noghri are quick and agile fighters. A Noghri gains Running Attack as a bonus Feat at 1st level, provided he or she meets the prerequisites of the Feat (minimum Dexterity of 13).

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Noghri do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Noghri have an extremely strong sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Noghri ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Noghri trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Noghri have an extremely strong sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Noghri ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Noghri do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: All Noghri gain the Martial Arts I feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Quick Reflexes
traits.description: A Noghri may choose to reroll any Initiative check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

Nosaurian.abilityText: All Nosaurians receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Nosaurian reflexes are quick, but their demeanor is abrasive toward other Species.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skill
traits.description: A Nosaurian treats the Climb skill as a Class Skill, regardless of Heroic Class.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Nosaurians has a Natural Weapon, in the form of sharp horns. When a Nosaurian makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapon, dealing 1d6 points of piercing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Nosaurians are always considered armed with their Natural Weapon.

traits.name: Survival Instinct
traits.description: A Nosaurian can choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Talz.abilityText: All Talz receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Winters of Alzoc III make the Talz strong and resilient, but they have had little chance to learn and develop intellectually.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Talz learn how to use technology very quickly. A Talz who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Talz ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Talz has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Talz makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Talz are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

Togorian.abilityText: All Togorians receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Togorians are strong and tough, but not terribly bright or learned.

traits.name: Feline Reflexes
traits.description: Togorians gain a +1 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Togorians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Physically Intimidating
traits.description: When using the Persuasion skill to Intimidate, Togorians use their Strength bonus instead of their Charisma bonus when determining the check modifier.

Togruta.abilityText: All Togrutas receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Togrutas are quick on the hunt, but more delicate than some Species.

traits.name: Pack Hunter
traits.description: Togrutas deal an additional 2 points of damage on melee attacks against opponents they Flank.

traits.name: Sneaky
traits.description: A Togruta can choose to reroll any Stealth check to Sneak, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Spatial Awareness
traits.description: Togrutas can sense their surroundings using passive echolocation. Togrutas ignore all Cover and Concealment when making Perception checks to Notice Targets within 10 squares.

Whiphid.abilityText: All Whiphids receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Whiphids are typically strong, but many lack experience in the wider galaxy.

traits.name: Cold Resistance
traits.description: Their thick fur gives allows Whiphids to add a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense to resist Extreme Cold.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Whiphid has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Whiphid makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with that attack, instead of normal Unarmed damage. Whiphid are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Whiphids have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Whiphids ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

Yarkora.abilityText: All Yarkora receive +2 bonuses to both their Wisdom and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Yarkora are charismatic and street smart.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skill
traits.description: Yarkora are adept at collecting information from a variety of sources. Gather Information is always a Class Skill for Yarkora.

traits.name: Confusion
traits.description: Once per encounter as a Standard Action, a Yarkora can make a Deception check against the Will Defense of all targets within 6 squares. If the Deception check result equals or exceeds a target's Will Defense, that target does not Threaten any squares until the end of the Yarkora's next turn. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

traits.name: Deceptive
traits.description: Naturally gifted at manipulation, a Yarkora can choose to reroll any Deception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Balosar.abilityText: All Balosars receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Charisma, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Constitution and Wisdom. Balosars are nimble and attentive, but are often in despair and a state of malnutrition.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: While their Antennapalps are extended, a Balosar may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. A Balosar may choose to retract their Antennapalps as a Free Action (extending them is likewise a Free Action) to pass as Human (no Deception check required).

traits.name: Mental Fortitude
traits.description: Balosars gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Deception and Persuasion checks, as they are extremely savvy in a variety of illicit fields.

traits.name: Toxic Resistance
traits.description: Growing up on a polluted homeworld has given Balosars resistance to toxic substances. Balosars receive a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against Poisons and Toxic Atmospheres.

Blood Carver.abilityText: All Blood Carvers receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Blood Carvers feel shunned by society due to their reputation as fearsome assassins.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Blood Carver with Acrobatics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Acrobatics) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Deathstrike
traits.description: Whenever a Blood Carver makes a melee attack against an opponent that is denied its Dexterity bonus to its Reflex Defense, the Blood Carver's attack, if successful, deals +1 die of damage.

Clawdite.abilityText: All Clawdites receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Clawdites are frailer than other Species, but are extremely adept at masking their appearance.

traits.name: Shapeshift
traits.description: Clawdites are shapechangers and can alter their appearance at will (see the Deception skill). While Shapeshifted, a Clawdite gains a +10 Species bonus on Deception checks made to disguise appearance. Additionally, a Clawdite can disguise its appearance as a Full-Round Action at no penalty. Clawdites cannot alter their size without the Metamorph feat.

traits.name: Startle
traits.description: Once per encounter, as a Reaction to being attacked, a Clawdite can make a Deception check against the target's Will Defense. If successful, the attacker takes a -5 penalty on their attack roll.

Falleen.abilityText: All Falleen receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Falleen posses uncommon magnetism and great interpersonal skills.

traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.description: Falleen are at home in air or water. A Falleen can hold their breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times their Constitution score before they need to start making Endurance checks.

traits.name: Pheromones
traits.description: As a Standard Action, a Falleen can make a special attack against any adjacent creature. The attack modifier is equal to the Falleen's Character Level plus their Charisma modifier. If the attack equals or exceeds the target's Fortitude Defense, that target moves -1 step along the Condition Track. If the attack fails to beat the target's Fortitude Defense, the target is immune to the Falleen's Pheromones for 24 hours. When the target is reduced to a total of -5 steps, instead of Falling Unconscious, the target's Attitude improves to Friendly. Pheromones function as an Inhaled Poison. Any creature holding its breath is unaffected (see Endurance for more details). A creature can be affected by Falleen Pheromones only once per round. Multiple exposures to Pheromones from different Falleen in the same round produce no additional effects. A Hostile creature cannot be targeted by this effect.

traits.name: Pheromone Acclimation
traits.description: Falleen gain a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against the Pheromones of other Falleen.

Gand.abilityText: All Gand receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Gand society values wisdom and cunning, but their off-putting appearance and strange manner make them difficult to understand.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Gand ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Hardy
traits.description: Gand are tough and resilient, capable of shrugging off injuries that would cripple other Species. Whenever a Gand uses their Second Wind, they regain a number of additional Hit Points equal to 5 + its Character Level.

traits.name: Limb Regeneration
traits.description: A Gand can regrow a lost limb in 1d10 days. At the end of this time, all Persistent penalties associated with the loss of the limb are removed.

traits.name: Special Equipment
traits.description: The dependence of Gand on the atmosphere of their homeworld restricts their ability to leave without use of specialized equipment. Without a Breathing Apparatus, a Gand would Suffocate. Without a Transliterator, a Gand cannot communicate in any language other than Gand. A Breathing Apparatus costs 2000 credits, and a year's supply of breathing filters cost 200 credits (50 credits on Gand). A Transliterator costs 100 credits. A Gand character begins play with these items at no cost.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Gand do not treat Gand Weapons as Exotic Weapons, instead treating them as a base item (for instance, a Gand Blaster Rifle would still be treated as a Rifle).

Houk.abilityText: All Houk receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Houk are huge and muscular, but have a reputation for their brutality and aggravation that is not undeserved.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Houk are thick-boned and thick-skinned. They gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Physically Intimidating
traits.description: Houk use their Strength modifier instead of their Charisma modifier for Persuasion checks made to Intimidate others.

Jawa.abilityText: All Jawas receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Jawas are quick and agile, but they lack the strength of larger creatures.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Jawas ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Desert Dweller
traits.description: When making Survival checks to resist the effects of Extreme Heat, Jawas can reroll the check, keeping the better of the two results.

traits.name: Scavenger
traits.description: Whenever a Jawa armed with an Ion weapon successfully damages a Droid or Vehicle, the Jawa adds half their Character Level in Ion damage.

traits.name: Tinkerer
traits.description: A Jawa can choose to reroll any Mechanics check, but must keep the second result, even if it is worse. In addition, Mechanics is always a Class Skill for Jawas.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Jawa trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Desert Dweller
traits.description: Jawas may Take 10 on Survival checks in desert terrain.

traits.name: Tinkerer
traits.description: A Jawa can choose to reroll any Mechanics check made to Repair an object, vehicle, or droid, but must keep the second result, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Jawas ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

Ryn.abilityText: All Ryn receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma. Ryn are gifted entertainers and performers.

traits.name: Connections
traits.description: Ryn benefit from an extensive information network that spans the galaxy. Whenever they travel, they can contact other Ryn to learn rumors and guarded information more easily than other races. Whenever a Ryn makes a Gather Information check to Learn Secret Information, they can forgo the credit cost. If this check fails, the Ryn can try again, but must spend the credits as normal.

traits.name: Musical Obfuscation
traits.description: Ryn can move air through their beaks to create pleasant tunes and notes. A Ryn can reroll any Deception check to convey Deceptive Information, keeping the better of the two results. A Ryn can project their sounds out to a range of 10 squares. As a Swift Action, a Ryn can Create a Diversion to Hide.

traits.name: Prehensile Tail
traits.description: Ryn have long tails nearly as strong and flexible as their hands. A Ryn can effectively hold (but not wield) a Small or smaller item with its tail. When holding a Medium or larger item with its tail, the Ryn is Encumbered and considered to be carrying a Heavy Load.

Toydarian.abilityText: All Toydarians receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Toydarians are weaker than Humans, but they are shrewd and accustomed to moving quickly to seal the deal.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Toydarians that have Deception as a Trained Skill gain the Skill Focus (Deception) feat as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Flight
traits.description: Toydarians are capable of flight, but cannot do so if they are carrying a Heavy Load.

traits.name: Force Resistance
traits.description: Toydarians receive a +5 bonus to their Will Defense against any use of the Use the Force skill.

Ubese.abilityText: All Ubese receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Ubese have slight build, and they are quick and agile.

traits.name: Aggressive
traits.description: If an Ubese hits with a Charge attack, the +2 bonus on attack rolls against the target persists for the duration of the encounter.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Ubese do not trust other cultures, so they are more suspicious of aliens. An Ubese with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Survival Instinct
traits.description: Ubese are tough and resilient, capable of withstanding the perils of even hostile worlds. An Ubese can reroll Survival checks, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Special Equipment
traits.description: Ubese cannot tolerate the atmospheres of other worlds and must rely on breath masks to survive. Without a breath mask, an Ubese begins to Suffocate. When not on their homeworld, Ubese wear environmental suits to stave of the Hazards found on other planets as well as to hide their appearance. An environmental suit functions as a Combat Jumpsuit that also grants a +2 Equipment bonus to Fortitude Defense. The suit also includes a voice modulator to emulate the voice of a male or female or to mask the Ubese's gender. An Ubese environmental suit with a breath mask and voice modulator costs 2000 credits, and a year's supply of breathing filters costs 200 credits (50 credits on Ubese). Ubese characters begin play with these items at no cost.

Wroonian.abilityText: All Wroonians receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Wroonians are graceful and swift, but often their physical prowess lags behind other Species.

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Wroonians are stubborn and hard to be deterred, giving them a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

traits.name: Soothing Voice
traits.description: A Wroonian may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Dug.abilityText: Dugs receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Dugs possess excellent reflexes and hand-eye coordination, but they rarely think before they act and are known for their crude demeanor.

traits.name: Athletic
traits.description: A Dug can reroll any Climb or Jump check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Dug can Take 10 on Climb and Jump checks even when distracted or threatened.

Gen'Dai.abilityText: All Gen'Dai receive a +4 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Gen'Dai are incredibly resistant to damage, but their long life spans cause them to be mentally unstable.

traits.name: Fast Healing
traits.description: Whenever a Gen'Dai takes its Second Wind, it regains 5 Hit Points every round at the end of its turn until killed, the end of the encounter, or until reaching one-half Hit Points (whichever comes first).

traits.name: Hibernation
traits.description: A Gen'Dai can enter a state similar to a Hibernation Trance. A Gen'Dai can use the Force Trance application of the Use the Force skill Untrained (and even if not Force-sensitive) and can remain in the trance indefinitely. A Gen'Dai in such a state ceases to Age, and needs no food, water, or air until it emerges from Hibernation.

traits.name: Mental Instability
traits.description: As they Age, Gen'Dai begin to lose touch with reality. For each Age Category a Gen'Dai advances past Adult, it takes an additional loss of -1 Wisdom. Gen'Dai characters never gain increased Wisdom due to Age, though they can increase their Wisdom scores as their Character Level increases.

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.description: A Gen'Dai can regrow lost body parts- including significant portions of its physical structure and individual limbs- in 1d10 minutes. At the end of that time, all Persistent penalties associated with the loss of one or more limbs are removed.

traits.name: Resilient Physiology
traits.description: Because of their unique physiology, the Gen'Dai can shrug off the effect of wounds that would be fatal to any other Species. Because of this, a Gen'Dai's Damage Threshold is doubled after adding bonuses for Feats and size when determining whether the Gen'Dai is killed by an attack (but not for any other purpose).

Geonosian.abilityText: All Geonosians receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Geonosians are deceptively strong, but their hive-like mentality makes it difficult for them to act on their own.

traits.name: Flight
traits.description: Geonosians are capable of flight, but cannot do so if they are carrying a Heavy Load.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Geonosians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Radiation Resistance
traits.description: Geonosians receive a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against Radiation. In addition, Geonosians never gain a Persistent Condition from the effects of Radiation.

Iktotchi.abilityText: Iktotchi receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Though they are a hardy Species, the Iktotchi are a quiet and introspective people and have trouble forming friendships.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Iktotchi have tough skin that provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Limited Precognition
traits.description: Iktotchi are gifted with limited precognition and are subjected to prophetic dreams. Once per encounter, as a Reaction, an Iktotchi can grant themself or any Vehicle the Iktotchi Pilots a +2 Species bonus to Reflex Defense against any single attack.

traits.name: Expert Pilot
traits.description: An Iktotchi can reroll any Pilot check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Kaleesh.abilityText: All Kaleesh receive a +2 bonus to both their Constitution and Charisma scores, but suffer a -2 penalty to both their Dexterity and Intelligence scores. Kaleesh are sturdy and work well with others, but lack sophistication and precision.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Kaleesh are a nomadic people who have learned to live off the land. A Kaleesh with Survival as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Survival) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Because of the thermoreceptor glands next to their eyes, Kaleesh ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Driven
traits.description: Kaleesh are focused individuals who maintain sight of their goals even under adverse conditions. Because of this single-minded drive, they gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Mind-Affecting effects.

traits.name: Persistent
traits.description: A Kaleesh can reroll any Endurance check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Kaminoan.abilityText: All Kaminoans receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Kaminoans are smart, largely emphasizing on education, but their arrogance tends to blind them to their flaws.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Kaminoans are expert cloners and posses remarkable knowledge about the physiologies of Species across the galaxy. A Kaminoan with Treat Injury as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Treat Injury) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Resilient
traits.description: Scientific refinements to their genetic code makes Kaminoans resistant to hazards that target their physiology. Kaminoans gain a +1 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Kaminoan trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Ocean Dweller
traits.description: Kaminoans may Take 10 on Survival checks in aquatic terrain.

traits.name: Reroll
traits.description: Kaminoans can choose to reroll any Treat Injury or Knowledge (Life Sciences) check, but must keep the second result, even if it is worse.

Kerkoiden.abilityText: Kerkoiden receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Though less hardy than other Species, Kerkoiden are devious and cunning.

traits.name: Predator's Heritage
traits.description: Despite their stature, Kerkoiden are descended from predatory beings. Kerkoiden gain a +2 Species bonus on damage rolls against a target that has been damaged since the beginning of the Kerkoiden's last turn.

traits.name: Social Cunning
traits.description: Kerkoiden may use their Wisdom modifier in place of their Charisma modifier for determining their bonus on Deception and Persuasion checks.

Nautolan.abilityText: All Nautolans receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Nautolan are tough, but also reckless and foolhardy.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Nautolans can't drown in water.

traits.name: Damage Reduction
traits.description: The rubbery hide of a Nautolan provides a Species bonus of Damage Reduction 2.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Nautolan may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Nautolan may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Nautolans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Pheromonal Sensor
traits.description: A Nautolan can reroll any Perception check made to Sense Deception or Sense Influence, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. If underwater, the Nautolan can take the better of the two results instead.

Nelvaanian.abilityText: All Nelvaanians receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Nelvaanians are thoughtful and patient, but are primitive and rooted in tradition.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Nelvaanians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Nelvaanians do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Nelvaanians have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Nelvaanians ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

traits.name: Shadow Swiftness
traits.description: If a Nelvaanian begins its turn with Concealment from a target or with the target unaware of it, the Nelvaanian does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity when moving through the target's threatened space until the end of the Nelvaanian's turn.

Republic Clone.abilities: Republic Clones begin play with the following Ability Score array: Strength 15, Dexterity 13, Constitution 10, Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 8. Additionally, a Republic Clone player can choose one Ability Score to increase by 2 at the time of Character Creation.
Republic Clone.abilityText: Republic Clones begin play with the following Ability Score array: Strength 15, Dexterity 13, Constitution 10, Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 8. Additionally, a Republic Clone player can choose one Ability Score to increase by 2 at the time of Character Creation.

abilityChoice.description: Republic Clones begin play with the following Ability Score array: Strength 15, Dexterity 13, Constitution 10, Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 8. Additionally, a Republic Clone player can choose one Ability Score to increase by 2 at the time of Character Creation.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Republic Clones gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

traits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
traits.description: Republic Clones are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. A Republic Clone character chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

Vurk.abilityText: All Vurks receive +2 bonuses to both their Constitution and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Vurks are resilient and reliable, but their thick fingers lack fine motor movement.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Vurks can't drown in water.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Vurk may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Vurk may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Placid
traits.description: Vurks are renowned for their ability to remain calm and collected, even in the face of danger. Once per encounter, as a Free Action, a Vurk can remove one Fear effect currently affecting it.

traits.name: Survival Instinct
traits.description: As nomads, Vurks are accustomed to moving around, even through dangerous places. A Vurk may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

Advozse.abilityText: All Advozsec receive a +2 bonus to both their Constitution and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Advozsec are cynical and hardy, though they lack a certain grace possessed by others.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: An Advozse can choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Advozsec ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Advozsec have a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

Chagrian.abilityText: All Chagrians receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Chagrians are physically strong, but lack grace.

traits.name: Breath Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Chagrians can't drown in water.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Chagrians ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Radiation Resistance
traits.description: Chagrians receive a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against Radiation.

traits.name: All Codru-Ji possess six limbs
traits.description: four arms and two legs. In addition, juvenile Codru-Ji hardly resemble their parents as much as they resemble small, furred, hexapedal canines. Referred to as Wyrwulves, Codru-Ji children continue to grow and develop in their six-legged form until puberty, when they pupate in cocoons and emerge as bipedal adults.

Codru-Ji.abilityText: None. Codru-Ji are skilled in many crafts, and are widely versatile.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Codru-Ji gains the Dual Weapon Mastery I feat as a bonus Feat provided he or she meets the prerequisites of the Feat (minimum Dexterity of 13 and Base Attack Bonus of +1).

traits.name: Extra Arms
traits.description: Codru-Ji can hold up to four items or Weapons at a time. This ability does not grant extra attacks; however, it does mean a Codru-Ji can wield two two-handed weapons at a time.

traits.name: Skilled Grappler
traits.description: Due to their extra arms, Codru-Ji gain a +5 Species bonus to both Grab and Grapple checks.

Klatooinian.abilityText: All Klatooinians receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Intelligence. Life on Klatooine has made the Klatooinians hardy, but they are slow to figure things out and often rely on others to guide them.

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Klatooinians are resolute individuals and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

Nagai.abilityText: All Nagai receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Nagai are charismatic and agile, but physically frail.

traits.name: Lightning Reflexes
traits.description: Nagai are fast on their feet and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

traits.name: Soothing Voice
traits.description: A Nagai may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Nagai treat Tehk'la Blades as Simple Weapons (Melee) instead of as Exotic Weapons (Melee).

Sakiyan.abilityText: Sakiyans are notable versatile for a Species, and as such receive no bonuses or penalties to their Ability Scores.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Sakiyans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Sakiyan with Perception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Lightning Reflexes
traits.description: Sakiyans gain a +2 Species bonus to their Reflex Defense.

Vahla.abilityText: All Vahla receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. The Vahla are naturally bright and shrewd, but they are physically frail.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Vahla receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Dark Side Stigma
traits.description: Whether they know it or not, each member of the Vahla Species is forever bound to The Dark Side of The Force. Vahla must spend 2 Force Points to reduce their Dark Side Score by 1 (instead of just 1 Force Point).

traits.name: Heightened Agility
traits.description: Vahla skeletons are primarily made of cartilage instead of bone, giving them a more flexible physique. A Vahla can choose to reroll any Acrobatics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Weequay.abilityText: Weequay receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Weequay are resilient but not particularly bright, and they don't relate well to others.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Weequay have learned to survive the harsh conditions on Sriluur. A Weequay with Endurance as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Endurance) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Expressive Pheromones
traits.description: Weequay communicate silently with other members of their tribe by emitting complex pheromones. This form of communication has a maximum range of 20 squares. Species with the Scent Species Trait can detect Weequay pheromones by smell, but this does not allow them to understand what the Weequay are communicating.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Weequay posses a thick, leathery hide that provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to his or her Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

Yuuzhan Vong.abilityText: Yuuzhan Vong receive a +2 bonus to Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to Wisdom. Yuuzhan Vong are powerfully built, but rash and quick to pass judgment.

traits.name: Biotech Proficiency
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong suffer no penalties while wielding Yuuzhan Vong Biotech.

traits.name: Force Immunity
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong can't take the Force Sensitivity feat, can't make Use the Force checks, and never gain Force Points. In addition, they are immune to any Force effect that targets their Will Defense (including Force Powers and aspects of the Use the Force skill).

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles), as starting Feats at 1st level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong treat Amphistaffs as a Simple Weapon (Melee) instead of as an Exotic Weapons (Melee).

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Yuuzhan Vong trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Force Immunity
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong can't take the Force Sensitivity feat, can't make Use the Force checks, and never gain Force Points. In addition, they are immune to any Force effect that targets their Will Defense (including Force Powers and aspects of the Use the Force skill).

traits.name: Technophobic
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong take a -5 penalty on attack rolls and skill checks made when using mechanical weapons or tools.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Yuuzhan Vong with the Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons) feat are considered proficient with the Amphistaff.

Zeltron.abilityText: Zeltrons receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Zeltrons get along well with others, yet they are prone to fits of passion.

traits.name: Empathy
traits.description: Zeltrons add their Charisma bonus (if any) to Perception checks made to Sense Deception or Sense Influence.

traits.name: Soothing Pheromones
traits.description: A Zeltron gains a +5 Species bonus on Persuasion checks made to Change Attitude. This bonus does not apply to other Zeltrons, as Zeltrons are acclimated to each other's pheromones.
