Celegian.abilityText: All Celegians receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Celegians are a thoughtful Species, but their unique physiology makes them less agile than others.

traits.name: Flight
traits.description: Celegians are capable of flight, but cannot do so if they are carrying a Heavy Load.

traits.name: Broadcast Telepath
traits.description: Celegians can use the Telepathy application of the Use the Force skill Untrained, even if they do not possess the Force Sensitivity feat. A Celegian automatically succeeds on this check to communicate with willing targets within 60 squares of it and in its line of sight.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Celegian may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Celegian may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Special Equipment
traits.description: Unless encased in a Celegian Life-Support Chamber, Celegians Suffocate.

Shard.abilityText: All Shards receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Shards have powerful minds, but must rely on somewhat clumsy Droid Bodies. A Shard retains its Constitution score, even when inside a Droid Body.

traits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
traits.description: Shards gain Use Computer as a bonus Trained Skill, regardless of their Class.

traits.name: Droid Shell
traits.description: Shards must inhabit Droid Bodies to travel and function in the galaxy at large. In this form, they gain the following traits: Shards must undergo Maintenance, as per a Droid. Shards are immune to Poison, Radiation, noncorrosive Atmospheric Hazards, and Vacuum. They can regain Hit Points only through the Repair Droid application of the Mechanics skill. They do not benefit from Natural Healing, unless they have the Force Repair Talent. Shards are considered cybernetic creatures for the purposes of Ion Damage. Shards can make use of Droid systems and accessories, subject to some limitations. Shards can have the following Droid Systems, with a Cost Modifier of x1: Appendages Droid Armor Communications Systems Compartment Space Sensor Systems Translator Units

Vultan.abilityText: All Vultans receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Vultans are creative and inquisitive, but their whimsical tendencies can often get the better of them.

traits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
traits.description: A Vultan can choose one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The Skill must be chosen from the Vultan's list of Class Skills.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Vultan with one or more Knowledge skills as Trained Skills gains a bonus Skill Focus feat for one Knowledge skill in which they are Trained.

Barabel.abilityText: All Barabels receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Wisdom. Barabels are tough and physically gifted, but lack grace and tend to be rather gullible.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Barabels ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Intimidating Presence
traits.description: A Barabel may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate others, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: A Barabel's thick scaly hide provides a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor Bonus.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Barabel has Natural Weapons in the form of sharp claws, needle-sharp teeth, and a thick tail. When making an Unarmed attack, a Barabel can choose to use Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage (Claws), piercing damage (Bite), or bludgeoning damage (Tail) with that attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Barabels are always considered armed when attacking with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Barabels do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Radiation Resistance
traits.description: A Barabel's armored hide protects them from hazardous Radiation, granting the Barabel a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense to resist the effects of Radiation. In addition, Barabels never gain a Persistent Condition from the effects of Radiation.

traits.name: Stun Resistance
traits.description: A Barabel's reflective scales defuse the effects of Stun weapons, granting them a +5 Species bonus to their Damage Threshold to resist the effects of Stun Damage.

Dashade.abilityText: All Dashade receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Dashade are remarkably agile given their impressive size, but they have difficulty recognizing threats and interacting with others because of their sense of superiority and their secretive nature.

traits.name: Environmental Adaptation
traits.description: Because of the unforgiving nature of their homeworld, Dashade gain a +5 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense against the effects of Extreme Temperatures and Radiation.

traits.name: Force Resistance
traits.description: Dashade receive a +5 bonus to their Will Defense against any use of the Use the Force skill.

traits.name: Frightening Visage
traits.description: A Dashade may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate, keeping the better of the two results.

Lurmen.abilityText: All Lurmen receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Lurmen are pacifists and healers, lacking the physical strength of other Species.

traits.name: Expert Climber
traits.description: Lurmen are great climbers and may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Mender
traits.description: Lurmen have learned to heal wounds rather than create them. A Lurmen Trained in Treat Injury reduces the Treat Injury DC for all applications of the skill by his or her Wisdom modifier.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Lurmen do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Roller
traits.description: A Lurmen can curl into a ball and quickly roll along the ground. As a Swift Action, a Lurmen can increase his or her base Speed by 4 squares. In this form, the Lurmen can only take the Move, Withdraw, Catch a Second Wind, Drop an Item, Recover, and Run Actions. The Lurmen can also spend a Swift Action to emerge from their rolling form, losing the +4 Speed bonus and regaining access to all Actions he or she can normally take.

Taung.abilityText: All Taung receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Taung are physically resilient, able to endure when others would give up.

traits.name: Armor Training
traits.description: Taung characters increase the Maximum Dexterity bonus from worn Armor by +1.

traits.name: Superior Defenses
traits.description: Because they constantly test themselves both physically and mentally in combat well before reaching adulthood, Taung gain a +1 Species bonus to all their Defenses.

traits.name: Warrior Culture
traits.description: Taung can reroll any Knowledge (Tactics) check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Thakwaash.abilityText: All Thakwaash receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Charisma. Thakwaash have large, powerful builds, but their multiple personalities make relations with other Species difficult.

traits.name: Irrepressible Mind
traits.description: Once per encounter, as a Free Action at the start of his or her turn, a Thakwaash can negate or remove any Mind-Affecting effects.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: Thakwaash gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

Trianii.abilityText: All Trianii receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom. Trianii have excellent vision and, because of their curious nature, are very aware of their surroundings.

traits.name: Heightened Agility
traits.description: A Trianii can reroll any Acrobatics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Trianii's sharp claws serve as Natural Weapons. When a Trianii makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Trianii are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Prehensile Tail
traits.description: A Trianii's long, flexible tail is dexterous and strong. A Trianii's tail can effectively hold (but not wield) a Small or smaller item. When holding a Medium or larger item with his or her tail, a Trianii is encumbered and is considered to be carrying a Heavy Load.

Yevetha.abilityText: All Yevetha receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Yevetha are strong despite their gaunt physiques, but their fatalistic culture and violent xenophobia color their outlook.

traits.name: Mechanical Aptitude
traits.description: Yevetha have a knack for technology. Once per encounter, a Yevetha can treat any single Mechanics check as if the die roll is a Natural 20.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Yevetha's retractable claws are Natural Weapons. When a Yevetha makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. A Yevetha is always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Xenophobia
traits.description: Yevetha have an intense distrust of all Species other than their own, and gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Persuasion checks made to improve their Attitude by any creature of a different Species.

Yuzzem.abilityText: All Yuzzem receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. Yuzzem are physically powerful, but their volatile nature earns them few allies.

traits.name: Expert Climber
traits.description: A Yuzzem can reroll Climb checks, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Yuzzem's large tusks and sharp claws serve as Natural Weapons. When making an Unarmed attack, a Yuzzem can use Natural Weapons, dealing 1d8 points of piercing damage (Bite), or slashing damage (Claws), instead of normal Unarmed damage. Yuzzem are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Physically Intimidating
traits.description: Yuzzem use their Strength modifier instead of their Charisma modifier for Persuasion checks made to Intimidate others.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: At close range (within 10 squares), Yuzzem ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and they take no penalty from poor visibility when Tracking.

Zygerrian.abilityText: All Zygerrians receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Zygerrians are durable, but are considered reckless by other Species.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Zygerrians receive the Martial Arts I feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Zygerrians have above-average willpower, gaining a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

traits.name: Strength of Conviction
traits.description: Zygerrians can use their Charisma modifier in place of their Wisdom modifier when calculating their Will Defense.

Bith.abilityText: All Bith receive a +2 bonus to both their Intelligence and their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Bith bodily processes are not very efficient, but members of the Species are bright and personable.

traits.name: Evolved Intellect
traits.description: Whenever a Bith spends a Force Point for any reason, the Bith gains a temporary Force Point, that can only be spent to add to an Intelligence-based Skill Check. If the temporary Force Point has not been used by the end of the encounter, it is lost.

traits.name: Meditative Trance
traits.description: Bith can enter into a Trance that lasts for four consecutive hours. At the end of the Trance, they gain the benefits of having rested for eight consecutive hours.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Bith have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Bith ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Bith trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Evolved Intellect
traits.description: Once per encounter, a Bith can treat any single Intelligence-based skill check as though it were a natural 20 on the die.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: A Bith can choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

traits.name: Meditative Trance
traits.description: Bith gain all of the benefits of resting for 8 hours after 4 hours of meditation, including natural healing and removing persistent conditions.

Defel.abilityText: All Defel receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Defel have slight builds but keen, probing minds.

traits.name: Partial Invisibility
traits.description: The fur of a Defel helps bend all but ultraviolet light, rendering him or her nearly invisible. A Defel in shadowy illumination or darker conditions is treated as having Total Concealment. Defel are fully visible and gain no Concealment against opponents with Darkvision.

traits.name: Special Equipment
traits.description: The eyes of Defel have evolved in such a way that they can see only in the ultraviolet spectrum. Defel that leave Af'El must wear special ultraviolet visors to see in normal light. Without such a visor, a Defel is considered Blind. An ultraviolet visor costs 2000 credits. A Defel character begins play with this item at no cost.

Elomin.abilityText: All Elomin receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Elomin have trouble relating to other Species, but they are exceptionally good at a number of intellectual pursuits.

traits.name: Driven
traits.description: Elomin gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense against Mind-Affecting effects.

traits.name: Impose Order
traits.description: Elomin are exceptionally focused on order, and strive to ensure that their chaotic companions are in the place where they are supposed to be. Once per encounter, an Elomin can spend a Swift Action to allow each ally within 12 squares to move up to half that ally's Speed as a Reaction. This movement does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity.

Fosh.abilityText: All Fosh receive +2 bonuses to their Dexterity and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Fosh are nimble and persuasive, but they possess frail and delicate bones.

traits.name: Deceptive
traits.description: A Fosh may reroll any Deception check, but must accept the result of the reroll, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Healing Glands
traits.description: Fosh can exude a powerful fluid from their tear ducts that can aid in the healing process for most Species. A Fosh is always considered to have a Medpac or Medical Kit for the purposes of making Treat Injury checks, and can perform Trained Only applications of the Treat Injury skill even when Untrained.

Givin.abilityText: All Givin receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Givin lack physical strength, but they are extremely analytical.

traits.name: Damage Reduction
traits.description: The hard shells of Givin provides a Species bonus of Damage Reduction 2.

traits.name: Deductive Reasoning
traits.description: Givin constantly evaluate data, discarding inconclusive results. A Givin can reroll any Knowledge check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Great Fortitude
traits.description: Givin gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

traits.name: Vacuum Immunity
traits.description: Givin have sealed exoskeletons and do not breathe in a conventional sense. They are immune to the effects of Vacuum and to the effects of inhaled chemicals and Poisons.

Gotal.abilityText: Gotals have no Ability Score adjustments, having no particular advantage or disadvantage in mentality or physical prowess.

traits.name: Energy Reading
traits.description: Gotals can read the emotions of any sentient being within 6 squares. As a Standard Action, a Gotal can make a Perception check against a target's Will Defense. A successful check reveals the target's emotional state (such as happy, sad, nervous, or scared).

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Gotals ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Sensor Cones
traits.description: A Gotal can use the Sense Force and Sense Surroundings applications of the Use the Force skill Untrained.

Herglic.abilityText: All Herglics receive a +4 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -4 penalty to their Dexterity. Herglics are large and strong, but they lack grace and manual dexterity.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Herglics are gregarious creatures with a gift for inducing others to open up. A Herglic with Gather Information as a Trained Skill receives Skill Focus (Gather Information) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Sturdy
traits.description: Herglics are solidly built and literally have tough skin. Whenever a Herglic catches a Second Wind, he or she regains a number of additional Hit Points equal to 5 + one-half its level.

Mrlssi.abilityText: All Mrlssi receive +2 bonuses to their Intelligence and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Mrlssi are small creatures, but they are quick-witted and very charismatic.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skill
traits.description: Mrlssi have a natural inclination for working with mechanical devices. Mechanics is always a Class Skill for Mrlssi.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Mrlssi love intellectual discussions. A Mrlssi with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Scholarly Inclination
traits.description: At 1st level, a Mrlssi gains any one Knowledge skill as a bonus Trained Skill.

Neimoidian.abilityText: Neimoidians receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Neimoidians avoid physical activity and confrontation, preferring instead to rely on the power of their personalities to achieve success.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feats
traits.description: Neimoidians strive to slant deals in their favor, and they make agreements that they can break easily if they feel so inclined. A Neimoidian with Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat. A Neimoidian Trained in Persuasion gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Neimoidian trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feats
traits.description: A Neimoidian with Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Deceptive
traits.description: A Neimoidians may choose to reroll any Deception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Nyriaanan.abilityText: All Nyriaanans receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Nyriaanans are calm and thoughtful, but rarely interact with outsiders.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skill
traits.description: Survival is always considered a Class Skill for Nyriaanans.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Like their Human ancestors, Nyriaanans are versatile and gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Nyriaanans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Pa'lowick.abilityText: All Pa'lowicks receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Pa'lowicks are a contemplative Species, but their lack of muscle mass hampers their physical prowess.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: A Pa'lowicks may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, a Pa'lowicks may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.description: Pa'lowicks are at home either in air or in water. A Pa'lowick can hold his or her breath for a number of rounds equal to 25 times his or her Constitution score before needing to make Endurance checks to hold his or her breath.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Pa'lowicks do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

Umbaran.abilityText: All Umbarans receive a +2 bonus to both their Dexterity and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Constitution. Umbarans are not as sturdy as many Species, but they are naturally stealthy and observant.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Umbarans are born observers, and they enjoy watching events from hidden vantage points. An Umbaran with Gather Information as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Gather Information) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Umbarans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Sneaky
traits.description: An Umbaran can choose to reroll any Stealth check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Umbaran trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Umbarans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: An Umbaran with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Soothing Voice
traits.description: An Umbaran may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Light Sensitivity
traits.description: An Umbaran exposed to bright light (such as sunlight) is Blinded for 1 round.

Altiri.abilityText: Altiri have no Ability Score adjustments.

traits.name: Bonus Trained Skill
traits.description: Altiri are versatile and accomplished at many tasks. An Altiri chooses one additional Trained Skill at 1st level. The skill must be chosen from the character's list of Class Skills.

traits.name: Visually Striking
traits.description: Altiri have a visually surprising or stunning appearance. An Altiri's Charisma bonus is doubled when they use the Persuasion skill on Humans and Near-Humans.

Anzati.abilityText: Anzati have no Ability Score adjustments.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skills
traits.description: Stealth and Survival are always Class Skills for Anzati.

traits.name: Energy Drain
traits.description: As a Swift Action once per round, an Anzati can use its proboscis to drain energy from a Grabbed target, moving the target -1 step on the Condition Track as a Persistent Condition, and granting the Anzati Bonus Hit Points equal to 5 + half the Anzati's Character Level. The Anzati can repeat this action each round until the target escapes the Grab, or until the target is rendered unconscious. If the target survives the feeding attempt, the Persistent Condition is removed after the target rests for 8 hours.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Anzati ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Mesmerize
traits.description: As a Standard Action, an Anzati can make a special attack against the Will Defense of a single target within 6 squares of the Anzati. The attack modifier is equal to the Anzati's Character Level plus his or her Charisma modifier. If the attack is successful, the target loses its Standard Action for its next turn. If the attack fails, the target is immune to this effect for 24 hours. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

traits.name: Presence Sense
traits.description: The Anzati's acute senses enable them to sense a person's luck. When making a Survival check to Track a living creature, an Anzati gains a Species bonus equal to the quarry's Force Point total.

Anarrian.abilityText: Anarrians have no Ability Score adjustments.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Anarrians gain one bonus Feat at 1st level.

traits.name: Strength Surge
traits.description: Once per encounter, an Anarrian can perform one of the following actions: Reroll one Strength-based Ability Check or Skill Check using a doubled Strength modifier (minimum +1) Add +2 to one damage roll when making a successful melee attack to which a Strength bonus is normally added (this benefit does not apply when the Weapon Finesse feat is used)

Chadra-Fan.abilityText: All Chadra-Fans receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Chadra-Fans are quick and precise, but their curiosity often overrides their sense of self-preservation.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Chadra-Fans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: A Chadra-Fan may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Quality Repairs
traits.description: When a Chadra-Fan uses the Repair or Jury-Rig applications of the Mechanics skill, the target Droid or device regains an additional 1d8 Hit Points.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Chadra-Fan trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Chadra-Fans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Tinkerer
traits.description: A Chadra-Fan may reroll any Mechanics check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse. Whenever a Chadra-Fan Repairs or Jury-Rigs a droid or a device with a Mechanics check, the droid or device regains an additional 1d8 hit points. Additionally, Mechanics is always a class skill for a Chadra-Fan.

Ebruchi.abilityText: All Ebruchi receive +2 bonuses to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Wisdom and Charisma. Ebruchi are strong and physically resilient but arrogant and repulsive to most other Species.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Ebruchi are naturally tough and resilient. Ebruchi gain Improved Damage Threshold as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Ebruchi are headstrong and stubborn. They gain a +2 Species bonus to their Will Defense.

traits.name: Unsettling
traits.description: Most Species find Ebruchi physical traits and habits unsettling. Once per encounter, when an Ebruchi successfully uses the Persuasion skill to Intimidate a target, the Ebruchi can move the target -1 step on the Condition Track instead of applying one of the standard Intimidation effects. The target must be within 6 squares of the Ebruchi, and the effect lasts until the end of the encounter. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

Ishi Tib.abilityText: All Ishi Tib receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence. Ishi Tib are problem-solvers, striving to find the most efficient solution, with a minimum drain to their resources.

traits.name: Breathe Underwater
traits.description: As amphibious creatures, Ishi Tib can't drown in water.

traits.name: Ecological Awareness
traits.description: Due to their conscientiousness and drive to protect the natural environment of their homeworld, an Ishi Tib can choose to reroll any Knowledge (Life Sciences) check, keeping the better of the two results.

traits.name: Expert Swimmer
traits.description: An Ishi Tib may choose to reroll any Swim check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. In addition, an Ishi Tib may choose to Take 10 on Swim checks, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Ishi Tib ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: An Ishi Tib has a Natural Weapon, in the form of a sharp beak. When an Ishi Tib makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of piercing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Ishi Tib are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

Killik.abilityText: All Killiks receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Intelligence. Killiks are a hardy but intensely focused Species.

traits.name: Hive Mind
traits.description: Killiks share a Hive Mind and use their antennae to communicate by telepathy with other members of their Species. A Killik within 1 kilometer of another Killik can communicate with it via Telepathy.

traits.name: Joiners
traits.description: Whenever a Killik reduces a living creature with an Intelligence score of 3 or higher to 0 Hit Points, or the Killik knocks the creature unconscious by moving it down the Condition Track, the Killik can transform the target into a Joiner. The target must be Helpless for this to occur. The process takes 1 minute and exposes the target to mind-altering pheromones. Once the target becomes a Joiner, its Attitude shifts to Friendly to the Killik and its allies, and the target gains a +10 bonus to its Will Defense against attempts to use Persuasion to alter its perceptions of its new Killik friends. A target transformed into a Joiner in this manner can make a DC 20 Intelligence check once per day to remove the effect and return to its original Attitude. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Killiks ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Multiple Limbs
traits.description: Killiks have four limbs that are generally used as arms and hands. When making multiple attacks, a Killik reduces the penalty to its attack rolls by 2.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: A Killik's chitinous exoskeleton provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to its Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Killik has Natural Weapons, in the form of three-fingered claws. When a Killik makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Killiks are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Killik sizes typically range from Small to Huge, with a few reaching the extremes of Fine and Colossal. When creating a Killik, apply the appropriate size modifiers per the table below.

traits.name: Additional Traits
traits.description: To include additional traits, see the Creature Generator. SIZE ABILITY MODIFIERS BASE SPEED REFLEX MODIFIER STEALTH MODIFIER DAMAGE THRESHOLD MODIFIER UNARMED DAMAGE Fine +8 Dex, -8 Str 4 Squares +10 +20 +0 1 Diminutive +6 Dex, -6 Str 4 Squares +5 +15 +0 1d2 Tiny +4 Dex, -4 Str 4 Squares +2 +10 +0 1d3 Small +2 Dex, -2 Str 4 Squares +1 +5 +0 1d4 Medium None 6 Squares +0 +0 +0 1d6 Large +8 Str, +8 Con, -2 Dex 6 Squares -1 -5 +5 1d8 Huge +16 Str, +16 Con, -4 Dex 4 Squares -2 -10 +10 2d6 Gargantuan +24 Str, +24 Con, -4 Dex 4 Squares -5 -15 +20 3d6 Colossal +32 Str, +32 Con, -4 Dex 4 Squares -10 -20 +50 4d6 Krevaaki Krevaaki are a peaceful, spiritual Species. With minimal interest in acquiring wealth, power, or fame, they seek wisdom through exploration and meditation. Although they are an ancient spacefaring Species, Krevaaki are rarely encountered in the galaxy at large. Even during The Old Republic Era- their most active period- groups of more than a few Krevaaki are seldom seen at any one location. Krevaaki In response to other Species' xenophobia, the Krevaaki cloak their tentacled bodies with heavy robes or similar garments, leaving only their two dominant tentacles exposed like humanoid arms. More than a few beings have been caught unaware by the sudden appearance of additional tentacles from beneath the folds of a Krevaaki's garments.

Krevaaki.abilityText: All Krevaaki receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Krevaaki are kind-hearted and spiritual, but their unusual physiques tends to unnerve other Species.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Krevaaki's tentacles give it an advantage in climbing. A Krevaaki who has Climb as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Climb) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Carapace
traits.description: Krevaaki gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense and a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Fortitude Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Stoic
traits.description: Krevaaki physiology and their limited ability to use facial expressions make them difficult for other Species to read accurately. Any creature attempting to use the Sense Deception or Sense Influence applications of the Perception skill on a Krevaaki takes a -5 penalty to the skill check.

traits.name: Grasping Tentacles
traits.description: Krevaaki use their two dominant tentacles like the arms and hands of a humanoid. Krevaaki gain a +2 Species bonus to Climb checks and a +2 Species bonus to Grab and Grapple checks.

variants.label: Alternate
variants.description: Alternate Krevaaki trait profile from the canonical species text.

traits.name: Carapace
traits.description: Krevaaki gain a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense and a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Fortitude Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Stoic
traits.description: Krevaaki physiology and their limited ability to use facial expressions make them difficult for other Species to read accurately. Any creature attempting to use the Sense Deception or Sense Influence applications of the Perception skill on a Krevaaki takes a -5 penalty to the skill check.

traits.name: Grasping Tentacles
traits.description: Krevaaki use their two dominant tentacles like the arms and hands of a humanoid. Krevaaki gain a +2 Species bonus to Climb checks and a +2 Species bonus to Grab and Grapple checks.

Lugubraa.abilityText: All Lugubraa receive a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Wisdom. Lugubraa are tough but lack the intelligence of most sentient Species.

traits.name: Accelerated Learning
traits.description: Before 50 years of age, a Lugubraa can have a maximum Intelligence score of 8. Around 50 years of age, a Lugubraa's Intelligence score naturally increases by 1d4+2 points.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: All Lugubraa gain the Improved Damage Threshold feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Although Lugubraa cannot see, their echolocation and heat detection abilities grant them the effects of Darkvision. Lugubraa ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Lockbite
traits.description: A Lugubraa that makes a successful Grapple attack can automatically latch onto a target with its teeth, giving the Lugubraa a +5 bonus to subsequent Grapple checks against the target.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Lugubraa has Natural Weapons, in the form of barbed tentacles. When a Lugubraa makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. Lugubraa are always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

Nikto.abilityText: All Nikto receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Nikto are tough and hardened, but their subservient status stunts their willpower and creativity.

traits.name: Great Fortitude
traits.description: Nikto gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense, having evolved to survive the harsh environment of their homeworld.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: A Nikto's tough hide provides a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Survival Instinct
traits.description: A Nikto can choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

O'reenian.abilityText: None. O'reenians are trained both in mind and in body, adapting to all circumstances.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: An O'reenian may choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Persistent
traits.description: An O'reenian can reroll any Endurance check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

Shistavanen.abilityText: All Shistavanen receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Shistavanen are lithe and agile, but their gruff demeanor isolates them from other Species.

traits.name: Bonus Class Skill
traits.description: Survival is always a Class Skill for a Shistavanen.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Shistavanen Trained in Perception gains Skill Focus (Perception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Shistavanen ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

traits.name: Natural Weapons
traits.description: A Shistavanen has Natural Weapons, in the form of sharp claws. When a Shistavanen makes an Unarmed attack, it may choose to use its Natural Weapons, dealing 1d6 points of Slashing damage with the attack instead of normal Unarmed damage. A Shistavanen is always considered armed with their Natural Weapons.

traits.name: Quick Reflexes
traits.description: A Shistavanen may choose to reroll any Initiative check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.

Sluissi.abilityText: All Sluissi receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Sluissi are slow and methodical, choosing to take their time with their work.

traits.name: Careful Worker
traits.description: A Sluissi who takes twice the normal amount of time required to use the Mechanics skill gains a +10 circumstance bonus to the Mechanics check.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Sluissi quickly learn to tinker with technology. A Sluissi who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains the Tech Specialist Feat as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Serpentine
traits.description: By coiling their serpentine bodies, Sluissi are able to make long jumps without getting a running start. Sluissi are always considered to have a Running Start for Jump checks. They also gain a +5 stability bonus when making Grapple checks against the Trip feat.

Sorcerer of Rhand.abilityText: All Sorcerers of Rhand receive a +4 bonus to their Intelligence, as well as a +2 bonus to their Wisdom. But they suffer -2 penalties to their Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma. Sorcerers lose their physical attributes in exchange for increased mental abilities.

traits.name: Beastly Traits
traits.description: A Sorcerer gains two Beastly Traits unique to his or her relationship with The Dark Side. Create these qualities (with approval from your Gamemaster), or select two using the Creature Generator.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Sorcerers of Rhand receive the Force Sensitivity feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Dark-Infused
traits.description: Sorcerers of Rhand gain the Force Training feat as a bonus Feat, but half of the Force Powers selected with this bonus Feat must have the [Dark Side] descriptor, and none can have the [Light Side] descriptor.

traits.name: Repulsive Appearance
traits.description: The Sorcerers of Rhand's repulsive physical appearances allow them to reroll Persuasion checks to Intimidate, and may keep the better result. They also gain a +5 bonus to their Will Defense when others attempt to use the Persuasion skill against them.

Squib.abilityText: All Squibs receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Squibs are flexible and light on their feet, but generally lack physical strength.

traits.name: Natural Diplomat
traits.description: A Squib gains a +5 Species bonus to Persuasion checks made to Haggle. Additionally, Persuasion is always a Class Skill for Squibs.

traits.name: Tech Savvy
traits.description: A Squib can reroll any Knowledge (Technology) check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse. Additionally, a Squib is not limited to Common Knowledge when making an Untrained Knowledge (Technology) check.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Squibs treat the Squib Tensor Rifle as a Rifle, rather than as an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

Ssi-Ruuk.abilityText: All Ssi-Ruuk receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Ssi-Ruuk are physically powerful but prone to panic and delay when faced with mortal danger, especially in space or on worlds that have not been consecrated.
special: The Tofs come from the darkest and most obscure portion of The Unknown Regions

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Ssi-Ruuk Trained in the Persuasion skill gain Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Force Blind
traits.description: Ssi-Ruuk cannot take the Force Sensitivity feat, cannot make Use the Force checks, and never gain Force Points. They can still increase their Dark Side Score and are still affected by Force Powers, Force Talents, and the Use the Force skill as normal.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: The naturally tough scales of the Ssi-Ruuk grant them a +2 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Silver Tongued
traits.description: Ssi-Ruuk use their keen sense of smell to detect changes in a target's emotional state. A Ssi-Ruuk may choose to reroll any Persuasion check, keeping the better of the two results.

traits.name: The Tofs come from the darkest and most obscure portion of The Unknown Regions
traits.description: the peripheral halo. Far removed from The Chiss Ascendancy, The Vagaari Empire, and The Ssi-Ruuvi Imperium in the galactic disk, the Tofs and others in the satellite galaxy they call Firefist (and named "Companion Besh" by Republic cartographers) have little understanding of, or sympathy for, traditional galactic power plays- with the exception of their aborted invasion shortly after the Battle of Endor. Tof-0 Their culture is intentionally anachronistic. Violently opposed to streamlined functionality, the Tofs have instead embraced elaborate dress and foppish ritual. Their interstellar vessels resemble traditional sailing ships and sport curved hulls and energy- radiating sails. Their royals wear tailored greatcoats and powdered wigs. Their soldiers carry cutlasses and cudgels and collect battle scars. Attire becomes more ornate as Tofs climb the military hierarchies that make up their society. Low-ranking deckhands wear simple cloth shirts, leather vests, and head-scarves. Ship's captains are outfitted with heavy buccaneer's boots, sparkling rings on nearly every finger, and a waist sash that holds a Blaster Pistol and a carved Club. Tofs do not bathe, covering their stink with perfumed bows on their tunics and boots. Males dominate Tof society. Females are seldom seen and generally kept on-planet where they are visited only when Tof vessels return to port. During long star voyages, Tof males exhibit reckless lust for humanoid females and often make unwanted moves on attractive targets (Or prove vulnerable to feminine flattery).

Tof.abilityText: All Tofs receive a +2 bonus to their Strength and Charisma, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Dexterity and Wisdom. Tofs are big, strong, and boisterous, but they often make poor choices in the name of sport and adventure.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Tofs are aggressive and chaotic combatants, firing and swinging wildly. A Tof can take either Rapid Shot or Rapid Strike at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Tof gains Mighty Swing as a bonus Feat at 1st level, provided he or she meets the prerequisites of the feat (minimum Strength of 13).

traits.name: Fearless
traits.description: Tofs are fearless on the battlefield. Tofs gain a +5 Species bonus to their Will Defense to resist Fear effects, as well as against the Intimidate application of the Persuasion skill.

Vagaari.abilityText: All Vagaari receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Vagaari are strong, but self-centered and hostile.

traits.name: Bioknowledge
traits.description: Vagaari have great working knowledge of biology throughout their sphere of influence in The Unknown Regions. A Vagaari may choose to reroll any Knowledge (Life Science) or Treat Injury check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Vagaari are accomplished actors and deceivers. A Vagaari that is Trained in the Deception skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Frightening Visage
traits.description: Vagaari spend most of their lives trying to intimidate others by presenting a threatening appearance. A Vagaari may choose to reroll any Persuasion check made to Intimidate, keeping the better of the two results.

Verpine.abilityText: Verpine are notably versatile for a Species, and as such receive no bonuses or penalties to their Ability Scores.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Verpine who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Heightened Awareness
traits.description: A Verpine can choose to reroll any Perception check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Hive Mind
traits.description: A Verpine can mentally communicate with any Verpine within a 1-kilometer range.

traits.name: Natural Armor
traits.description: A Verpine's carapace provides a +1 Natural Armor bonus to their Reflex Defense. A Natural Armor bonus stacks with an Armor bonus.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Verpine treat the Verpine Shattergun as a Pistol instead of an Exotic Weapon (Ranged).

Amani.abilityText: All Amanin receive a +2 bonus to their Strength, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Charisma. Amanin are a muscular Species, but their nomadic lifestyles make them difficult to connect with.

traits.name: Acceleration
traits.description: When using the Run Action, Amanin may roll into a ball to move at greater speed. When Running, Amanin may take a -5 penalty on Perception checks, and have their Speed doubled.

traits.name: Expert Climber
traits.description: Due to their arboreal background, Amanin may choose to Take 10 on Climb checks even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Amanin do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Reach
traits.description: Unlike most Medium-sized Species, Amanin have a natural Reach of 2 squares.

traits.name: Resilient
traits.description: Amanin gain a +1 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense due to their natural resilience.

Arcona.abilityText: All Arcona receive a +2 bonus to their Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Wisdom. Arcona aren't as perceptive as many other beings, but they are known for their ability to socially adapt to life in the galaxy at large.

traits.name: Great Fortitude
traits.description: Arcona gain a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense. Arcona have adapted to the harsh environment of Cona and can convert many different toxins into harmless chemicals.

traits.name: Scent
traits.description: Arcona have a keen sense of smell. At close range (within 10 squares), Arcona ignore Concealment and Cover for purposes of Perception checks, and take no penalty from poor visibility while Tracking.

Devaronian.abilityText: Male Devaronians tend to be more carefree and less personable than female Devaronians, but they are also more agile. All male Devaronians receive a +2 bonus to their Dexterity, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Wisdom and Charisma. All female Devaronians +2 bonuses to both their Intelligence and Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Devaronians are master manipulators. A Devaronian with Deception as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Deception) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Natural Curiosity
traits.description: Because of their natural curiosity, Devaronians are very attentive to small details that can reveal weaknesses. Once per encounter as a Standard Action, a Devaronian can make a Perception check against a single target within line of sight (DC 15 or the result of an opposed Stealth check, if the target is actively attempting to hide). If successful, the Devaronian gains a +1 insight bonus on attack rolls against that target.

Nazren.abilityText: All Nazren receive +2 bonuses to both their Strength and Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity and Charisma. Nazren are strong and hardy but taciturn and withdrawn; they shun the spotlight.

traits.name: Resilience
traits.description: Once per encounter, when a Nazren would be moved down the Condition Track by taking damage equal to or exceeding the Nazren's Damage Threshold, the Nazren may reduce the number of steps moved down the Condition Track by 1 (to a minimum of 0 steps).

Pau'an.abilityText: All Pau'ans receive +2 bonuses to both their Wisdom and Charisma, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Pau'ans leave most of the manual labor to their Utai neighbors, but they are capable leaders.

traits.name: Authority
traits.description: As a Species accustomed to being in authority, Pau'ans add a +1 bonus to any insight or morale bonuses they grant to themselves or other characters.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: A Pau'an with Persuasion as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Persuasion) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Pau'ans ignore Concealment (but not Total Concealment) from darkness.

Phindian.abilityText: Phindians receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Charisma. Phindians are sharp-minded, but typically anti-social.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: All Phindians gain Skill Training (Knowledge (Technology)) as a bonus Feat at 1st level.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Phindians that have Mechanics as a Trained Skill gain Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

Polis Massan.abilityText: All Polis Massans receive a +2 bonus to their Wisdom, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Strength. Polis Massans are thoughtful and ponderous, but their physique is largely lackluster.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Polis Massans are known across the galaxy for their skill with medicine. A Polis Massan with Treat Injury as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Treat Injury) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Polis Massans ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Limited Telepathy
traits.description: In conjunction with their sign language and technological methods of communication, Polis Massans are able to employ a weak form Telepathy that allows them to convey simple feelings and thoughts to any willing recipient within 10 squares. Polis Massans can use the Telepathy aspect of the Use the Force skill Untrained (even if not Force-sensitive). If the target of the Telepathy is within 10 squares, no skill check is required to initiate the telepathic contact.

traits.name: Mute
traits.description: Polis Massans have no vocal cords, and must rely on a combination of sign language, computers, and Telepathy in order to communicate with others. When they learn new Languages, they can understand but not speak them.

Skakoan.abilityText: All Skakoans receive a +2 bonus to their Intelligence, but suffer a -2 penalty to their Dexterity. Skakoans lack agility but have a rigidly logical, almost computerlike mind.

traits.name: Bonus Feat
traits.description: Skakoans receive the Armor Proficiency (Light) feat at 1st level as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Skakoans with Mechanics as a Trained Skill gain Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat.

traits.name: Mechanical Savant
traits.description: Once per encounter, a Skakoan can make a Standard Action Mechanics or Use Computer check as a Swift Action.

traits.name: Special Equipment
traits.description: Skakoans suffer limitations outside their native atmosphere. Without a Pressure Suit, a Skakoan takes 1d6 points of damage each round and begins to Suffocate. Skakoan characters begin play with a Light Pressure Suit at no cost.

Stereb.abilityText: All Stereb receive a +4 bonus to their Strength and a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to their Dexterity, Intelligence, and Charisma. Stereb are strong and hardy. They are not aggressive, and they might take time to reach a decision, but once they choose a course of action, they are deliberate and steadfast.

traits.name: Conditional Bonus Feat
traits.description: Stereb are very adept when working with stone. A Stereb who has Mechanics as a Trained Skill gains Skill Focus (Mechanics) as a bonus Feat. However, this bonus applies only when the Stereb character is working with stone.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Stereb ignore Concealment (including Total Concealment) from darkness. However, they cannot perceive colors in total darkness.

traits.name: Primitive
traits.description: Stereb do not gain Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons), Weapon Proficiency (Pistols), or Weapon Proficiency (Rifles) as starting Feats at first level, even if their Class normally grants them.

traits.name: Subterranean
traits.description: Stereb are adapted to a subterranean life and can Take 10 on Survival checks when in an underground environment, even when distracted or threatened.

traits.name: Tough
traits.description: Like the stone they work with, Stereb are physically hardened, gaining a +5 Species bonus to their Damage Threshold and a +2 Species bonus to their Fortitude Defense.

Tusken Raider.abilityText: Tusken Raiders receive a +2 bonus to their Constitution, but suffer -2 penalties to both their Intelligence and Wisdom. Tusken Raiders are hardy and fit, but are simple and quick to anger.

traits.name: Sneaky
traits.description: A Tusken Raider can choose to reroll any Stealth check to sneak, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

traits.name: Survival Instinct
traits.description: A Tusken Raider may choose to reroll any Survival check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse.
